Pretty backdrop, good looking lead characters, designer costumes, lavish homes, slow motion scenes and beautiful backdrop everything else is BS. If estrogen can have this attitude, what can t testosterone do! is a toxic dialogue repeated multiple times. The whole premise of the movie is glorifying dishonesty as it suits the main character. In a central confrontation scene he says never show your insecurities, fears and weaknesses to a woman who s not your mother, as it ll give them too much power this is such a regressive thought and so detrimental to a healthy open relationship of mutual respect and trust. I can t believe a woman has directed this toxic movie. The actors have done justice to their respective parts, it s really not their fault it s a very poor story with flawed characters. Highly impractical, unethical and idiotic. Can you imagine a lead female character trying to live with her husband and ex boyfriend, justifying and normalizing it ? The main character never admits he could be wrong, never takes anyone s advice and justifies his every move with batshit crazy logic. This is a super regressive movie.

War 2 is a full throttle cinematic ride that masterfully fuses adrenaline pumping action with heartfelt drama. From the very first frame, it hooks you in opening with stylish, larger than life introductions, then slowly building a world where every character matters. By the time the interval hits, the narrative kicks into overdrive, refusing to let go until the credits roll. Anil Kapoor turns in a sophisticated, multi layered act that lingers in your mind, while NTR Jr. sets the screen ablaze his fight choreography and dance sequences are pure dynamite. Hrithik Roshan, with his effortless charisma and commanding presence, once again proves why he s a superstar for the ages. Kiara Advani doesn t just appear in scenes she owns them, exuding elegance, fierceness, and a magnetic charm. The action set pieces are crafted with precision fierce, exhilarating, and visually breathtaking, yet never crossing into mindless brutality. Standouts include AI powered combat tricks and perfectly timed slow motion shots that could make your jaw drop. The background score and songs elevate every moment, occasionally sparking a nostalgic grin with shades reminiscent of Shaitaan a playful nod rather than imitation. And then there s the ending emotionally fulfilling yet cleverly teasing a bigger, bolder chapter ahead. It s the kind of finale that makes you leave the theater grinning, your heart still racing. In short, War 2 isn t just an action blockbuster it s a rollercoaster that makes you feel for its heroes and cheer for their victories. A thrilling, stylish, and emotionally resonant spectacle Final verdict Go nd watch yourself thn decide.

War 2 is watchable not bad, not great 😐. Hrithik and NTR look good on screen 💥 there are stylish visuals 🎥 and big action set pieces 💣 but with 400cr budget 💰, global locations 🌍 and two massive stars , it should ve been unforgettable and it just isn t. Biggest missed chance? 🎵 Songs and dance you ve got two of the best dancers in India 🕺🕺 and you give them Salame Ali and other forgettable tracks 🙄 with basic choreography. Imagine if they did an Ek Pal Ka Jeena Nachore Nachore remix face off Hrithik starts with his hook step 🎶 NTR comes in with his 🔥 they swap moves 🔄 end with a fusion step 💥 theatre goes crazy 🤯. That one scene would ve been replayed for weeks but nope wasted. The rest is safe 😴 template story 📜 no real spy planning 🧠 injuries that vanish like magic even a sniper bullet to the chest 💥 and next scene full fight 😂 random big scale set pieces 🚆🚗🚤 and then a random Kiara bikini shot 👙 why bad strategy if the only aim was to pull crowds instead of adding to the story. Verdict Fun for a one time watch 🎬 but you walk out thinking missed opportunity 💔.

Asalu eh films aina, big or small, honestly oka 2 3 days aagi, talk batti velladam better. There were days when people had a blast buying audio cassettes and CDs, then waiting to watch those films, now it is a very rare feat. Unwanted vfx, stupid fans, SM marketing, jobless people making reels of hook tunes, and finally producers hyping is actually killing the film. Business ni passion ani cheppi mandini mosam chestunaru. Most of the films lo creativity ledu, serious ness ledu, commitment ledu, music taste ledu. How long it has been, since anyone has seen a full satisfactory film? Like lead actors, comedians, villain, music, side artists have all come close to proper closure? People made films in the past tha have become super hits and made big money. These days films are being made to make big money by duping people. That is Major difference!!! Handful of directors like SRV, SSR, Anurag Kashyap, Vetrimaran, Vishal Bharadwaj, GVM make films with some strong intent whether hit or flop, all other filmmakers passively look like they wanna hit jackpot by cooking something. All music directors of today, are overrated IMO, partly due to the way money and fame flows, creative long lasting impressions have become scarce. Thanks a lot brother for being honest and strong. You guys are saving a lot of people from wasting money and time.

Ikkada twist enti antey dhani cost around 2 Billion USD antey rupees lo around 16,000 crores plus. Antha costly plane ni five members ni champadaniki vadatharu. Malli ah suquence okati. Antha kastapadam enduku direct oka missile vadilithey saripothundhi kada. 2. Bad VFX plus multiple people shoot at heroes not even a single bullet gets to them. 3. Heroes sliding down simply with there hands from Iron cables, kanisam gloves kuda undavu. Hands undala povala? 4. Having seen a few reviews, I feel they are all shallow, perfunctory, and sound bored. As the first sci fi universe in Indian cinema, Kalki deserved a lot better than this casual attitude. Hopefully, we will get deeper analysis videos once the spoiler phase is over. Yes, Amitabh is getting a lot of praise for his performance, but the important thing here is that a Telugu filmmaker and team gave him the platform to shine in a Prabhas vehicle. That is extraordinary, people are glossing over this as if it is a daily occurrence. There is no precedent for such a thing. Imagine SRK signing, say, Mohanlal in one of his films, then letting the latter outshine him. Another thing that people are neglecting to mention is that it took a South filmmaker to write something this powerful for Amitabh. Why are Hindi filmmakers unable to do this? Again and again South filmmakers are showing Bollywood all the things they are doing wrong. There are many many influences that the film is synthesizing in its quest for a modern, yet Indian spectacle, but I see no mention of them in reviews. The core story for example is very close to Children of Men. I am not using influence in a pejorative sense, it makes sense to me that we have to start with the influences we grew up with. In time, hopefully, our sci fi films and worlds will depart from Hollywood models. The movie is saddled with some technical flaws, I have seen complaints about the sound, songs, dubbing and so on. It was also dumped on a non holiday release date. All these hamper the heights the film can reach. Again not much discussion about this.

Bhayya the best about this video is Meeku ardham kaani oka point ni naakey ardham kaalapoyi undavachu ani annaru chusara unlike everyone else who treat their misunderstandings as directors mistake, however that might be true according to different dimension of thought as director is responsible for what he says and what we understand from the movie. Anyway that s a different topic, here I think I can tell what I understood as this scene is not very much related to the main plot and many people already revealed what s the prabhas character is already in some reviews I don t think we can hide much of it. I think the way and many people understood this particular scene is different from what Nag sir wanted to convey, you yourself are saying that Sumathi Deepika Padukone fell into that tube or whatever it is exceptional case, yes indeed but it s not because she s carrying god Kalki in her womb but the entire scenario is created by the spy herself as she mentioned there are a lot of ways to escape from that way even the people from the complex might not know but she knows as she had been there trying to reveal those, so don t relate the scene as if it happened because lord Kalki is in her womb if that s the case even that single drop extracted wouldn t happen and hence this entire sequence is the escape plan plotted by the spy. This is my understanding and maybe you re right many people might not understand that thing as the super natural events such as Gandeevam getting activated and these human plots to escape are shown in the same sequence without explaining the difference in occurrence. Nag sir should have considered all types of audience in these kind of sequences, rather he left it to audience relative capacity to interpret that, which again is relative to ones understanding according to me to tell that story in 2 50 hrs I think he had to consider these things as straight forward. And in my opinion this is one of the best movies I watched recently. Very happy to see Telugu movie growing and even happier to see directors are taking these kinds of risks.

My review on RRRMovie 1st 50 minutes of the movie took me to a brilliant cinematic experience. The bonding between the protagonists, their chemistry,two power packed songs. Everything was right till the beginning of intermission fight. The entry of N number of wild animals loaded in a truck is the foundation of a downhill ride. Surprisingly, RRR never picked up from that point.Only the bheem song on NTR is a relief in otherwise faulty script. Certainly, the master storyteller Rajamouli lost his way in the transit. The biggest letdown is,there are only powerful fights, no powerful scenes, leaving a few here and there. When the injured protagonist charan wakes up to dress like Alluri seetarama raju,for reasons best known to the director,is a laughable episode. For your information,he continues in the same costume till the very end. Does anyone remember a mediocre film named hanuman junction . There is a fight sequence,where arjun carries jagapati babu on his back and the duo cleanup the villain gang. I laughed uncontrollably during that funny scene.And Rajamouli gave me the similar funny moment with a prolonged carry on back fight. This is the only comedy sequence in RRR. Rest is a bunch of blood bath fights. In my opinion, movies are categorised as good, average and bad. No reference is needed about genres.A movie is a movie. We pay for tickets to get entertained, not to discuss about the strain and money involved in making a film. In the recent past, i am blessed to have seen pushpa and bheemla nayak . Two good,too good movies. And RRR didn t entertain me.The so called visual grandure goes to dust when not supported by a tight screenplay. For me, this overrated epic falls somewhere between good and bad. I call it Average . Note The performance by two lead actors is spot on.

RRR.. నిజంగానే 450 కోట్లు ఖర్చు పెట్టారు. నిజంగానే రాజమౌళి సూపర్బ్ గా డైరెక్ట్ చేశాడు. నిజంగానే ఇద్దరూ టాప్ హీరోలు యాక్టింగ్ చించేసారు. ఫైట్స్ ఇరగదీశారు. నిజంగానే VFX వర్క్ హాలీవుడ్ రేంజ్ లో ఉంది. ఇవన్నీ నిజాలే. కానీ ఇవన్నీ కలిపితే నిజంగానే RRR ఒక మంచి, ఫైనెస్ట్ సినిమాటిక్ ఎక్స్పీరియన్స్ ఇచ్చిందా అంటే.. నాకైతే ఇవ్వలేదు. ఇది గొప్పగా తీసిన సినిమానే కానీ గొప్ప సినిమా ఏమీ కాదు అనిపించింది. ఈ స్టోరీకి base ఏంటి అంటే.. ఒక చిన్న పిల్లను బ్రిటిష్ వాళ్ళు బలవంతంగా తీసుకెళ్తే, హీరో ఆ పిల్లను తిరిగి తీసుకురావడానికి ఆదిలాబాద్ నుండి ఢిల్లీకి వెళ్తాడు. మొత్తం స్టోరీ జరగడానికి కారణం అదే. కానీ బ్రిటిష్ వాడు ఆ పిల్లను ఎందుకు తీసుకుపోయాడో, అంత నష్టం జరుగుతున్నా సరే ఆ పిల్లను ఎందుకు వదిలిపెట్టడో, పోనీ ఎందుకు చంపేయడో ఏమి అర్థం కాదు. బ్రిటిష్ వాడు ఆయుధం లేని భారతీయుడిని చంపడానికి బుల్లెట్ వేస్ట్ చేయడు. కొట్టి చంపేస్తాడు. అని రెండు మూడు సార్లు సినిమాలోనే చూపించారు. కానీ దేనికీ పనికిరాని ఒక గోండు పిల్ల కోసం వందల మంది సైన్యాన్ని దింపి ఏదేదో చేస్తాడు. ఇదెక్కడి లాజిక్కో రాజమౌళికే తెలియాలి. Story Base లోనే పెద్ద బొక్క ఉంది. ఈ బొక్కను పట్టించుకోకపోతే ఫస్ట్ హాఫ్ అంతా చాలా బాగుంటుంది. ఇక సెకండ్ ఆఫ్ అంతా గాల్లో లేపాడు సినిమాను. ఎక్కడా భూమ్మీద జరుగుతున్నట్టు అనిపించదు. హీరోలకు మనకు తెలీకుండా ఏమైనా అతీత శక్తులు ఉన్నాయేమో? అనిపిస్తుంది. చచ్చిపోయెలా కొట్టించుకుంటారు. మళ్లీ వెంటనే లేస్తారు. ఒకడు undertaker. ఒకడు kane. గ్రాఫిక్స్ చేయొచ్చు కానీ సినిమా మొత్తం గ్రాఫిక్స్ మాత్రమే చేస్తే ఎలా? స్టోరీ వీక్ అయిపోదా? బాహుబలి అంటే రాజులు, రాజ్యాలు ఉన్న సినిమా కాబట్టి గ్రాఫిక్స్ చేశాడు అనుకుందాం. RRR సినిమా స్టోరీకి అన్ని గ్రాఫిక్స్ ఏం అవసరం రా? గ్రాఫిక్స్ ఎక్కువ పెట్టేస్తే చాలు హాలీవుడ్ సినిమా రేంజ్ కి వెళ్లిపోవచ్చు అనుకున్నాడేమో. ఇక, సినిమాలో ఉన్న మెయిన్ ఎలిమెంట్.. ఎమోషన్. ఎక్కువ అయితే ఏదైనా వెగటు పుట్టిస్తుంది. ఫస్ట్ హాఫ్ లోనే ఎమోషన్స్ పీక్స్ కి తీసుకెళ్ళాడు. మళ్లీ సెకండ్ ఆఫ్ మొత్తం ఎమోషన్స్ తప్ప ఇంకో ఎలిమెంట్ లేదు. ఎంత సేపు ఎమోషన్ ఫీల్ అవ్వాలి రా బాబు. బోర్ కొట్టేసింది. సినిమాలో రియాలిటీ ఎక్కడా కనిపించదు. బ్రిటిష్ వాళ్ళు ఎంత మంది వచ్చినా మన ఇద్దరు హీరోలు కొట్టేస్తారని మనకు అర్థం అయిపోతుంది. ఇంకేముంది. సెకండ్ ఆఫ్ చూస్తున్నంత సేపు ఏ మాత్రం ఇంట్రెస్ట్ రాదు. పైగా 3 గంటలు సినిమా. అయిపోయే సరికి హెడేక్ వచ్చేసింది. ఇద్దరినీ సమానంగా చూపించాలి అని సీన్లు పెంచుకుంటూ పోయాడు. రామరాజు కారెక్టర్ బ్రిటిష్ వాళ్ళతో ఫైట్ చేయడానికి cause మంచిగా అనిపిస్తుంది. మధ్యలో భీమ్ వచ్చి అనవసరంగా లేనిపోని పెంట చేస్తాడు. అది కూడా ఆ చిన్నపిల్ల కోసం. 🤦 వీళ్ళ ఇద్దరి ఎమోషన్ నే భరించలేక చస్తుంటే మళ్లీ అజయ్ దేవగన్ తో ఇంకో అరగంట ఎమోషన్. లాస్ట్ కు వచ్చేసరికి ఎమోషన్ పెరిగి పెరిగి లూస్ మోషన్ పట్టుకున్న ఫీలింగ్ కలుగుతుంది. క్లైమాక్స్ లో ఒక బైక్ స్టంట్ ఉంటాది. దీనమ్మ అడవిలో నుండి ఎన్టీఆర్ బైక్ విసిరెస్తే రాకెట్ వెళ్లినట్టు వెళ్లి బ్రిటిషోడి కోటలో పైఅంతస్థులో పడుతుంది. 😭 మన సినిమాల్లో ఫిజిక్స్ ను కూనీ చేయడం చూసాం కానీ, రాజమౌళి ఏకంగా రాకెట్ సైన్సుకే ఎసరు పెట్టాడు. రాజమౌళి ఫీట్ల గురించి పక్కన పెట్టీ స్టోరీ విషయానికి వస్తే.. అది స్టోరీ కాదు.. ఒక పెద్ద సైజు నవల. ఇప్పుడు అర్థం అయ్యింది రాజమౌళి ముందు 2 ఛాన్స్ ఎక్కడ ఉంది. కీరవాణి మ్యూజిక్, బ్యాక్ గ్రౌండ్ స్కోర్ బాగానే ఇచ్చాడు కానీ కుప్పలు కుప్పలుగా ఎలివేషన్ సీన్లు పెట్టుకుని ప్రతి సీను లేపమంటే ఏ మ్యూజిక్ డైరెక్టర్ అయినా ఏం చేస్తాడు. అందుకే కీరవాణి కొన్ని చోట్ల సో సో గా కొట్టేసాడు. మొత్తానికి సినిమా గురించి ఒక్క మాటలో చెప్పాలి అంటే.. ఎమోషన్ ఓవర్ డోస్ విత్ సర్కస్ ఫీట్స్ అండ్ గ్రాఫిక్స్ క్రిటిక్స్ కూడా రాజమౌళి మార్క్ ప్రమోషన్ మాయలో పడి బొక్కలు This man wrote the perfect review of this film I m just copying it because I agree with everything he wrote CRINGE is the word to best describe this stage play. So much hype for such bad film. The plot is so weak that the emotions look completely forced. Firstly they get the tribal girl for no valid reason as her tattoo skills or the singing skills were not shown. And i dont know how do they justify that a son of a patriot working for the british and hitting his fellow contrymen in order to fulfil his promise to his father, i mean what kind of a dumb logic is it. A whole british army getting scared by an unknown guy from the forest without any weapons or skills but the animals which he caught but couldnt even train them. artwork looks so conjested and looks like watching some theatre play. Executing anything and everything and calling it cinematic liberty. As always britishers roles r so bad. Showing multiple times that Plant based medicine can make you completely recover from literally anything like a deadly snake venom or broken legs with in minutes or hours. You can grow a real long hair with in a very short period. A person from the a forest tribe coming straight from a forest to the city and working as a motorcycle mechanic with utmost skill. Honestly Very tough to stand the first half, then comes the second half just to test whether you r alive or no. The interval block animal chaos sequence is nothing but real badly executed amature stuff with cringe worthy cg. Such shabby cg work for this so called magnum opus was never expected. A guy singing without autotune while getting belted on a public platform was something only indian films can pull off. Calling a snake with simple taps on a the floor!! Finding a person who was kept in a solitary cell with simple taps on the ground!! hero pulling off the door of a solitary cell with bare hands, is that a joke? Offer a lift to a girl and she ll leave anything for u and come with you no matter who you are. Its better not to mention the music or the songs. Place a stone in a slingshot and use it in reverse, thats RRR for u. Taking years and execiting such a bad film like this is not acceptable coz theres SARDAR UDHAM with the same pre independence setup and the whole film is executed in just 8 months and the output is on par to the world class, which excelled in each and every department. If am not wrong the amount which was said to be 500 cr is misleading and they said that just to create the hype, coz you can hardly see that amount on screen. Cant blindly support any bad film coz of the names attached to it. Dont care about who s casted in a film or who made it, A bad film is a bad film.

I just watched Adipurush in 3D. I already knew that many NPC s would not like this film, but personally, I prefer this modern version over the old versions. I also think that many of these NPC s didn t even pay attention to the disclaimer. Maybe it is because they re programmed NPC s, haha! Filmmakers made it clear how they intended to present the characters from the Ramayana by releasing teasers, trailers, and songs before the film s release. However, these hallucinating NPC s stubbornly clung to their outdated beliefs, thinking they actually witnessed Lord Ram and the other characters in real life, haha! These NPC s are quick to upgrade themselves with fashionable clothing, glasses, shoes, and expensive cars, but they refuse to imagine and visualize their beloved Gods in these new interpretations. Considering that Lord Ram has been patiently listening to the boring desires of these devotees for generations, I hope he took a break from their monotonous wishes and enjoyed watching Adipurush with other gods, and perhaps even liked the film. Jai Shree Ram!

As an average telugu cinema lover, I can give a better review than this half baked review. I ll start with the negative aspects. Yes, there were a few technical things like dubbing and abrupt cuts that didn t seem well. But if you re not a reviewer, it won t bother you much. At few points the story seemed predictable. The positive aspects Story was good. Yes. It was. Anushka carried the film beautifully. All the other actors also did a great job. Songs and bgm were also great. There were a LOT of hype scenes. A heroine fighting bad guys so unapologetically was extremely refreshing to see. Overall, had a fun experience watching the movie. Worth watching atleast once in the theatres. I know reviews can be very subjective. But the movie is definitely not as bad as reviewers making it seem to be.
